title: HFSP Research Grant — Program Grant
description: High-risk, high-reward basic life science research through international teams. India-based PIs can lead or join. One of the most prestigious international grants for life sciences.

title: IGSTC WISER — Women in Science & Engineering Research (Indo-German)
description: Promotes collaboration between women researchers in India and Germany. Paired mode only — one Indian woman PI + one German woman PI required. Covers research staff, consumables, travel, and capacity building.

title: ICGEB Arturo Falaschi SMART Fellowship (Mar 2026)
description: Scientific Mobility for Advanced Research Training. 3–9 months at any ICGEB Member State lab (not home country). Open to PhD students and postdocs (PhD ≤5 years). Stipend USD 800–2,000/month + bench fee + travel.

title: ICGEB Short-term PhD Fellowship (Mar 2026)
description: 1–12 months at ICGEB labs in Trieste (Italy), New Delhi (India), or Cape Town (SA). For collaborative predoctoral research. Indian applicants may apply to Trieste or Cape Town (not New Delhi — home country rule). Monthly stipend + travel + health insurance.

title: ICGEB Biosecurity Fellowship — Biological Threat Training (Mar 2026)
description: 2–6 months at ICGEB labs (Trieste, New Delhi, Cape Town). Focus: molecular detection of biological threats, epidemiology, genomic surveillance, drug discovery, vaccine development, diagnostics. Co-funded by Norwegian Agency (UNODA/BWCISU). Directly relevant to AMR.

title: ICGEB CRP Research Grant 2026
description: ICGEB Collaborative Research Programme (CRP). Supports original research in basic science, human healthcare, industrial/agricultural biotechnology and bioenergy. India is a member state — up to 3 CRP + 2 Early Career Return grants per call. International collaboration mandatory.
eligibility: Researcher at ICGEB Member State institution

title: ICGEB Early Career Return Grant (CRP)
description: Part of ICGEB CRP call. For young PIs returning to ICGEB Member States after postdoctoral training abroad. Supports setting up independent labs. Up to 2 awards per member country per call.

title: Indo-Swiss Joint Research Programme (ISJRP) — ICMR/DBT/SNSF
description: Joint call by ICMR + DBT (India) and Swiss National Science Foundation (SNSF). Indian PI funded by ICMR/DBT; Swiss PI funded by SNSF. Focus: biomedical, health, and life sciences research. Live deadline: May 5, 2026.

title: DBT-BIRAC Joint Call for Proposals on ‘Bio AI’ for Fostering High Performance Biomanufacturing under BioE3 Policy Last Date :30th June,2026...
description: 2 DBT-BIRAC Joint Call for Proposals on ‘Bio AI’ for Fostering High Performance Biomanufacturing under BioE3 Policy Last Date :30th June,2026... Opens on 24th November 2025 Last Date of Submission 30-06-2026

title: Fulbright-Nehru Doctoral Research Fellowship
description: 6–9 months dissertation research in the United States. Priority: AI, energy, space science, defense.

title: Fulbright-Nehru Postdoctoral Research Fellowship
description: 8–24 months at a US institution. For Indian early career researchers in science, technology, and innovation fields.

title: EMBL Predoctoral Fellowship (PhD Programme)
description: Fully funded PhD positions at EMBL sites across Europe. Annual intake. Indian applicants eligible and encouraged.

title: ANRF Core Research Grant (CRG)
description: Supports research proposals in basic and applied sciences from faculty at Indian academic institutions.

title: DBT-BioCARe (Biotechnology Career Advancement)
description: Women scientist scheme for re-entry into mainstream S&T. Supports biotechnology research.

title: CSIR Symposia/Conference Grant
description: Supports organization of national/international conferences and symposia at Indian institutions.

title: ICMR Ad-hoc Research Projects
description: Extramural funding for biomedical and health research. Open to medical colleges, universities, research institutions.

title: BIRAC BIONEST (Bioincubator Nurturing Entrepreneurship)
description: Supports establishment of biotech incubators at academic institutions and research parks.

title: Wellcome India Alliance — Intermediate Fellowship
description: For Indian researchers with a strong track record seeking independence. Covers salary, research costs, training.

title: EMBO Installation Grant
description: Supports researchers returning to or establishing labs in EMBC member states and associate member countries including India.

title: Grand Challenges Explorations — Open Call
description: Supports bold ideas in global health and development. India-based researchers strongly encouraged.

title: NIH PAR — Exploratory/Developmental Research (R21)
description: Supports new, untested ideas and approaches. Collaborative grants with US PI may include Indian co-investigators.

title: IUSSTF Indo-US Joint Research Program
description: Joint research grants for Indian and US researchers. Covers exchanges, workshops, and collaborative projects.

title: PAR Foundation 2026 Grant — Beyond Antibiotics
description: 2026 theme: 'Beyond Antibiotics: Preventive Strategies That Lower the Need for Antibiotic Use.' Supports research reducing infection risk and improving antimicrobial stewardship. Global applicants welcome including India.

title: Google India Research Award
description: Supports faculty at Indian universities engaged in world-class research relevant to Google. Sub-track for early-career faculty (within 5 years). Annual call.

title: NCBS RDO Funding Database (Reference)
description: NCBS Research Development Office maintains a comprehensive, manually-curated database of Indian and international funding opportunities. Excellent supplementary reference.

title: DST-FIST (Fund for Improvement of S&T Infrastructure)
description: Infrastructure grant for upgrading S&T facilities at universities and colleges. Level I (up to ₹80L) and Level II (up to ₹2Cr).

title: DST-PURSE (Promotion of University Research and Scientific Excellence)
description: Grants to universities with good research output to strengthen research infrastructure and programs.

title: DST Science and Heritage Research Initiative (SHRI)
description: Research integrating modern science with India's ancient heritage — astronomy, mathematics, metallurgy, traditional medicine.

title: SERB-POWER Research Grant (Women Researchers)
amount: Up to ₹30 Lakhs (Level II — SASTRA eligible) / ₹60L (Level I)
description: Promotes women researchers in science and engineering. Two levels: Level I for IITs/IISc/NITs/Central Univ (up to ₹60L, 3yr); Level II for State/Private institutions like SASTRA (up to ₹30L, 3yr). Regular academic/research position required. All S&E frontier areas.

title: ANRF National Post Doctoral Fellowship (N-PDF)
description: Postdoctoral fellowship for Indian researchers. Provides mentor support at premier Indian institutes. Rolling applications.

title: SERB/ANRF Ramanujan Fellowship
description: For Indian scientists returning from abroad or working overseas to take up research positions in India. 5-year fellowship.

title: ANRF TARE (Teachers Associateship for Research Excellence)
description: Allows college teachers to work in nearby IITs/IISc labs. Bridges teaching and research, supports ≤3 years.

title: ANRF Start-up Research Grant (SRG)
description: For faculty who obtained PhD ≤5 years ago and are in first faculty position. Supports independent research setup. Rolling applications throughout year. Cannot simultaneously hold NPDF or TARE.

title: ANRF Prime Minister Early Career Research Grant (PM-ECRG)
description: New ANRF scheme replacing SERB-ECRA. Supports early career researchers at Indian institutions. INSPIRE Faculty, Ramanujan, and Ramalingaswami Fellows are explicitly eligible. Budget up to ₹1L/year each for travel and contingency.

title: ANRF SIRE — Short-term International Research Experience
description: Supports Indian researchers for short-term research visits (1–6 months) to overseas labs. For PhD students, postdocs, and early career faculty. Rolling applications.

title: ANRF Swarnajayanti Fellowship
description: Prestigious DST/ANRF fellowship for scientists below 45 years with exceptional track record. 5-year fellowship with complete academic freedom. Annual call; highly competitive (~15 awarded/year).

title: CSIR Junior Research Fellowship (JRF)
description: For NET-qualified candidates to pursue PhD in science. Awarded twice yearly (June & December cycle via CSIR-NET).

title: CSIR Senior Research Fellowship (SRF)
description: Upgrade from JRF after 2 years with good performance. Also direct SRF (Gates) scheme for experienced researchers.

title: CSIR Research Associateship (RA)
description: Postdoctoral fellowship at CSIR institutes or universities. For PhD holders within 3 years of degree award.

title: DBT Junior Research Fellowship (DBT-JRF)
description: For pursuing PhD in Biotechnology/Life Sciences. Selected via written test (BET). Twice yearly.

title: DBT Ramalingaswami Re-entry Fellowship
description: Prestigious re-entry fellowship for Indian scientists working abroad, to return and build independent labs in India. 5-year tenure.

title: DBT Research Associateship (DBT-RA)
description: Postdoctoral programme in biotechnology and life sciences. PhD holders within 3 years of degree award. Hosted at any recognized Indian institution with an established research group.

title: DBT-IYBA — Innovative Young Biotechnologist Award
description: Annual award + research grant for outstanding young researchers in biotechnology under 35 years. Provides 3-year research support in addition to the award. One of DBT's most competitive early-career recognitions.

title: DBT-BUILDER Programme
description: Biotechnology/Life Sciences University-Based Interdisciplinary Life Sciences Department and Research (BUILDER). Infrastructure and research support for universities to build interdisciplinary life sciences programs.

title: DST INSPIRE Faculty Award
description: 5-year tenure track fellowship for young Indian scientists under 32 to work in any Indian academic or R&D institution.

title: DST Women Scientist Scheme-A (WOS-A)
description: Research grants for women scientists who had a career break. Supports independent research for 3 years.

title: DST KVPY (Kishore Vaigyanik Protsahan Yojana) — Fellowship
description: Scholarship-cum-fellowship for students in BS/BTech pursuing basic sciences, to be encouraged into research careers.

title: ICMR Junior Research Fellowship (ICMR-JRF)
description: For pursuing PhD in biomedical and health sciences. Selected via ICMR-JRF exam held annually.

title: ICMR International Fellowship for Indian Biomedical Scientists
amount: Travel + monthly allowance (as per ICMR norms)
description: Supports Indian biomedical scientists to work at advanced research labs abroad for 3–6 months. Covers travel, maintenance allowance, and research costs. Annual call. Strong career development opportunity for mid-career researchers.

title: ICMR ANVESHAN — Small Research Grant (ICMR SMALL)
description: ICMR's new small grant scheme (rebranded). Investigator-initiated research proposals in biomedical and health sciences. Faster turnaround than ad-hoc grants. Follow-on ANVESHAN available for ongoing work.

title: ICMR NISCHA — Intermediate Research Grant
description: ICMR intermediate-scale grant for established researchers. Mid-tier between ANVESHAN (small) and full ad-hoc grants. For health research with clear translational potential.

title: Prime Minister's Research Fellowship (PMRF)
description: Flagship fellowship for BTech/MSc graduates joining PhD at IITs, IISc, NITs, IISERs. Twice yearly intake (Feb & July).

title: INSA Young Scientist Award
description: Annual award for Indian scientists under 35 for outstanding contributions. Comes with a medal, citation, and research support grant. One of the most prestigious young scientist recognitions in India.

title: DST Young Scientist Award (SERC Fast Track / SRG equivalent)
description: ANRF/SERB early career support for young scientists. Fast-track funding mechanism for scientists within 5 years of PhD. Superseded partially by SRG; check ANRF portal for current active scheme name.

title: NASI Young Scientist Platinum Jubilee Award
description: National Academy of Sciences India award for scientists under 35. Includes cash prize, medal, and visibility for early career researchers.

title: IASc-INSA-NASI Joint Summer Research Fellowship
description: Summer research fellowships for final-year BSc/BE/MSc students to work with fellows of the three science academies. Good pipeline for future PhD students; also useful for faculty seeking collaborative network.

title: ANRF International Travel Support (ITS)
description: Supports Indian researchers to present papers at international conferences abroad. Rolling applications throughout the year.

title: DBT Overseas Associateship
description: Supports Indian scientists with PhDs to work at overseas labs for 6–12 months. Covers travel, subsistence, and research costs.

title: DBT-EMBL Short-Term Visit Programme
description: Joint DBT-EMBL programme supporting short visits by Indian researchers to EMBL labs in Heidelberg and other sites.

title: CSIR Travel Grant for International Conferences
description: Financial support for scientists/researchers to attend/present at international conferences. Applications reviewed on a rolling basis.

title: INSA International Bilateral Exchange Programme
description: Exchange visits with foreign academies (Royal Society, NAS, JSPS, CNRS, DFG). For Indian scientists visiting abroad or hosting foreign scientists.

title: INSA Senior Scientist Fellowship
description: For INSA Fellows and active scientists post-superannuation to continue research. Provides honorarium and research support.

title: DST-NIDHI PRAYAS Grant (Proof of Concept)
description: DST National Initiative for Developing and Harnessing Innovations (NIDHI). PRAYAS supports proof-of-concept development for innovators with technology-based ideas. Up to ₹10L grant for prototyping at NIDHI-PRAYAS host institutes.

title: DST-NIDHI Technology Business Incubator (TBI)
description: Supports academic institutions in establishing technology business incubators. SASTRA could apply to host a TBI. Provides seed funding infrastructure, mentorship ecosystem, and connects startups to industry.

title: BIRAC Biotechnology Ignition Grant (BIG)
description: BIRAC's flagship early-stage support for biotech startups. Proof-of-concept funding for innovative biotech ideas. Open to individuals and companies. One of the most competitive biotech startup grants in India.

title: BIRAC-SPARSH (Social Innovation Grant)
description: BIRAC support for affordable and accessible innovations in healthcare and agriculture addressing underserved populations. Promotes frugal innovation and social entrepreneurship.

title: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS)
description: DPIIT scheme with ₹945 Cr outlay. Grants via eligible incubators for proof-of-concept, prototype development, product trials, and market entry. Up to ₹20L as grant + up to ₹50L as convertible debentures. Disbursed through incubators — SASTRA incubation centre applies.

title: MeitY TIDE 2.0 (Technology Incubation & Development)
description: MeitY Technology Incubation and Development of Entrepreneurs scheme. Supports IT/AI/IoT/Cybersecurity/FinTech startups through academic incubators. Two tracks: Incubator Support (₹3.15Cr) + Startup Grants.

title: ANRF PM Early Career Research Grant — Startup Research
description: UGC Start-up Research Grant for newly appointed faculty (within 3 years of joining). Available at UGC 2(f)/12B institutions. Covers basic sciences, medical sciences, engineering. For establishing independent research.

title: CEFIPRA Indo-French Joint Research Project (General)
description: CEFIPRA/IFCPAR bilateral programme. Indian PI funded by DST; French PI by ANR. Covers joint research, researcher exchanges, workshops. All STEM disciplines. Proposals submitted in parallel to CEFIPRA (India) and ANR (France).

title: CEFIPRA Indo-French ICT Research Programme
description: ICT-focused bilateral call. Indian PI submits via CEFIPRA online + hard copy to CEFIPRA New Delhi; French PI submits to Inria separately. Focus: AI, networks, security, systems.

title: IGSTC 2+2 Research Projects (Indo-German)
description: Indo-German Science & Technology Centre flagship programme. 2 Indian + 2 German partners (academia + industry on each side). Applied research, technology transfer focus.

title: IGSTC SING — Seed Networking Grant (Indo-German)
description: Seed funding for initiating bilateral India-Germany research connections. Rolling call. Covers preliminary experiments, consumables, short visits, conference registration. Low-barrier entry point for Indo-German collaboration.

title: DST-DFG Indo-German Joint Research (Science & Engineering)
description: Joint research projects between Indian (DST/ANRF-funded) and German (DFG-funded) PIs. Proposals submitted in parallel — Indian PI to DST, German PI to DFG via elan system. Both agencies must recommend funding.

title: DST-JSPS India-Japan Cooperative Science Programme (IJCSP)
amount: Mobility grant — travel, per diem, workshop costs
description: Joint research projects and workshops/seminars between Indian (DST) and Japanese (JSPS) researchers. Parallel submission required. Focus on mobility — exchange visits, seminars. Results notified Apr/May following year.

title: Newton-Bhabha Fund — Indo-UK Joint Research
description: UK-India bilateral fund managed by British Council and UKRI. Supports joint research, PhD placements, researcher exchanges, and workshops. Indian PI funded by DST/DBT/other Indian agency; UK PI by UKRI.

title: UKRI-DST India Joint Initiative (via GCRF/IUK)
description: Various UKRI calls explicitly open to India-UK partnerships. Indian partner typically funded by DST/DBT; UK partner by UKRI. Check UKRI opportunity finder filtered by 'India' for current calls.

title: IUSSTF Joint Research Projects (Indo-US)
description: Indo-US Science & Technology Forum. Indian PI funded by DST; US PI funded by NSF/NIH/DOE depending on theme. Also includes Virtual Network Centres and Joint Workshops.

title: MeitY-NSF Joint Research Programme
description: India-US bilateral for semiconductors, next-gen communications, cybersecurity, green tech, intelligent transport. Indian PI applies to MeitY; US PI to NSF. Single joint proposal, reviewed by NSF as coordinating agency.

title: DST-MOST Indo-Israel Joint Research (S&T)
description: Bilateral programme between DST (India) and Ministry of Science & Technology (Israel). Joint research in all S&T areas. Proposals submitted in parallel. Annual calls typically open Oct-Dec.

title: DST-NRF Indo-Korea Joint Research Programme
description: Joint research between Indian (DST) and Korean (NRF) researchers. Covers joint R&D projects and bilateral visits. Annual call.

title: DST-CSIRO India-Australia Strategic Research Fund
description: Joint bilateral fund between DST and CSIRO (Australia). Focus on areas of mutual interest: agriculture, water, clean energy, health. Indian PI funded by DST, Australian by CSIRO.

title: HFSP Long-Term Fellowship
description: 3-year postdoctoral fellowship in a different country/discipline. Strongly interdisciplinary. Annual call.

title: PAR Foundation 2026 Grant — Beyond Antibiotics
description: 2026 theme: 'Beyond Antibiotics: Preventive Strategies That Lower the Need for Antibiotic Use.' Supports AMR prevention research and educational initiatives. Global applicants including India eligible.

title: ICGEB Arturo Falaschi Postdoctoral Fellowship
description: Postdoctoral training at ICGEB labs in Trieste, New Delhi, or Cape Town. Average 2 years. Competitive package: stipend + health insurance + research costs. Top fellows eligible for Early Career Return Grants after completion.

title: ICGEB-JNU PhD Fellowship (India-specific)
description: PhD positions at ICGEB New Delhi in collaboration with JNU. Requires valid CSIR/UGC/ICMR/DBT/DST-INSPIRE/BINC JRF. Annual intake. Full stipend + world-class research environment.

title: Wellcome India Alliance — Early Career Fellowship
description: 5-year fellowship for researchers who have completed PhD ≤ 5 years ago. Covers salary, research costs, and training. Highly competitive.

title: Wellcome India Alliance — Intermediate Fellowship
description: For established Indian researchers seeking independence. 5-year award with salary and substantial research budget.

title: Wellcome India Alliance — Senior Fellowship
description: 5-year award for researchers with strong independent track record. Covers full research program costs.

title: Travel to EMBO officeTravel directions to the EMBO Offices in Heidelberg

title: EMBL Postdoctoral Fellowship (EIPOD)
description: EMBL Interdisciplinary Postdoctoral (EIPOD) programme at Heidelberg and other sites. Interdisciplinary life science research. Biannual calls.

title: EMBO Workshop Organizing Grant
description: Scientists can apply to organize an EMBO Workshop anywhere in the world, including India. Core funding up to €35,000 + €5,500 for participant travel grants + €1,000 childcare + €1,000 accessibility = up to €42,500. EMBO handles registration, abstract submission, and workshop webpage. Highly relevant for faculty hosting life science conferences.
eligibility: Any EMBO Member State or beyond

title: EMBO Lecture Course Organizing Grant
description: EMBO funds practical lecture courses in life sciences. Organisers apply to run intensive training courses. Supports curriculum, invited speakers, and participant scholarships. Can be hosted in India.

title: EMBL Course and Conference Travel Grant
amount: Variable cap per event (travel + accommodation + registration)
description: EMBL provides travel grants for researchers to attend EMBL/EMBO courses and conferences. Covers airfare, accommodation, visa, registration fees. Apply during abstract/motivation letter submission for each event. Selection based on scientific merit, location, career stage.

title: EMBO Postdoctoral Fellowship (Spring/Autumn Rounds)
description: Supports excellent postdocs for up to 2 years at labs in EMBC member states. International mobility required. Includes salary/stipend, relocation allowance, childcare support. From 2026: Spring round deadline = 4th Friday of January.

title: EMBO Scientific Exchange Grant
description: Supports short scientific visits to labs in EMBC member/associate countries. Rolling deadline, decisions within 6 weeks.

title: EMBO Short-Term Fellowship
description: Up to 3 months at EMBC member country lab. For PhD students and postdocs. Rolling applications.

title: FEBS Short-Term Fellowship
description: Supports short research visits (1–3 months) to a lab in a different country. Open to researchers from FEBS constituent societies.

title: FEBS Science for Life Laboratory Prize
description: Annual FEBS prize for outstanding contributions to molecular life sciences. Includes travel to FEBS Congress.
Travel grant for Indian PhD students/researchers. Source: AdhyaSuman/International_Travel_Grants (GitHub).

title: ACM-India: IARCS Travel Grants
description: Travel grant for Indian PhD students/researchers. Source: AdhyaSuman/International_Travel_Grants (GitHub). Contact: travelgrants@iarcs.org.in

title: ANRF: International Travel Support
description: Travel grant for Indian PhD students/researchers. Source: AdhyaSuman/International_Travel_Grants (GitHub). Contact: ms.its@serb.gov.in

title: CSIR Travel Grant
description: Travel grant for Indian PhD students/researchers. Source: AdhyaSuman/International_Travel_Grants (GitHub). Contact: tgsm@csirhrdg.res.in
Travel grant for Indian PhD students/researchers. Source: AdhyaSuman/International_Travel_Grants (GitHub).

title: ACM-RFG
description: Travel grant for Indian PhD students/researchers. Source: AdhyaSuman/International_Travel_Grants (GitHub). Contact: acmirfg@acmindia.org

title: AICTE Research Promotion Scheme (RPS)
description: Promotes research culture in AICTE-approved institutions. Two tracks: Basic/Applied Research (₹20L, 2yr) and Development Research (₹30L, 3yr). Less competitive than DST/DBT — underutilised by faculty at deemed universities. SASTRA is directly eligible. Submit via AICTE web portal.

title: AICTE Margdarshan — Faculty Development Grant
description: AICTE faculty development and capacity building grants. Covers short-term courses, workshops, and training programmes at premier institutions. Also includes Quality Improvement Programme (QIP) support.

title: AICTE-INAE (Indian National Academy of Engineering) Research Grant
description: Joint AICTE-INAE scheme for frontier engineering research. Includes Young Engineer Award track with research support. For engineering disciplines at AICTE institutions.

title: Georg Forster Research Fellowship (AvH) — India-eligible
description: For researchers from developing/transition countries (incl. India) to conduct research in Germany for 6–24 months. All disciplines. Highly prestigious. Rolling deadline — no fixed call. Strong publication record needed. Covers salary, travel, family allowance.

title: Humboldt Research Fellowship for Postdoctoral Researchers
description: 6–24 months at a German university/research institution. Open to Indian researchers with PhD ≤4 years. Any discipline. Rolling deadline — apply anytime. One of the most prestigious postdoc fellowships worldwide.

title: Humboldt Research Fellowship for Experienced Researchers
description: For senior researchers (>4 years post-PhD) to work in Germany for 6–18 months. All disciplines. Highly prestigious. Can be split visits. India has one of the highest success rates globally.

title: Humboldt Research Award
description: Prestigious award for internationally recognised researchers in any discipline. Nominated by German academics — cannot self-apply. Carries €60,000 prize and extended research stay in Germany.

title: Novo Nordisk Foundation Challenge Programme — Open Call
amount: Varies by call (up to DKK millions)
description: NNF Challenge Programmes tackle specific global health/life science problems. Some calls explicitly open to international (including Indian) collaborators. Focus areas: infectious disease, antimicrobial resistance, metabolic disease, data science for health. Check current open calls.

title: NNF Data Science Research Infrastructure Grant
description: Supports computational biology, bioinformatics, and data science research infrastructure. Increasingly open to global collaborations. India-based researchers with Danish collaborators are eligible.

title: Royal Society Newton International Fellowship
description: 2-year fellowship for early career non-UK researchers to work at UK institutions. All disciplines. India-eligible. Strong career development — alumni network is highly connected. Annual call.

title: Royal Society International Exchanges — Cost Share
description: Seed funding for new collaborations between UK and international researchers. India participates in the Cost Share scheme (funded by both RS and Indian agency). Covers short visits and workshops up to £12,000. Rolling deadline.

title: DAAD Research Stays for University Academics (India)
amount: Monthly allowance + travel (as per DAAD norms)
description: Short research stays (1–3 months) at German universities for Indian faculty. Covers travel and accommodation. No age limit. Also includes bilateral exchange for PhD students.

title: DST-DAAD Personnel Exchange Programme (PPP) — Indo-German
amount: Travel + per diem (DST norms for Indian side)
description: Bilateral MoU between DAAD and DST. Supports researcher exchanges for joint research projects. Indian PI applies to DST; German PI to DAAD. Covers travel + per diem for both teams. Well-established channel.

title: DAAD WISE Scholarship (Women in STEM)
description: For women engineering/science students from India for summer internships at top German universities. Highly competitive, well-regarded. Also increases visibility for follow-on collaborations.

title: Microsoft: MSR India Academic Outreach
description: Travel grant for Indian PhD students/researchers. Source: AdhyaSuman/International_Travel_Grants (GitHub). Contact: travelgrants@microsoft.com

title: Google: Conference Scholarships
description: Travel grant for Indian PhD students/researchers. Source: AdhyaSuman/International_Travel_Grants (GitHub). Contact: research-programs-in@google.com
